threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Stokey' instead of just 'The Nook' when suggesting a coffee spot. You'll learn soon enough!
Last Saturday on the 59, I overheard a guy confessing he once tried to pay with a bag of Percys. The driver didn’t even blink.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a flock of pigeons casually fighting over a bratwurst on the sidewalk while a couple argued over their favorite Kebab spot. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find pop-up clubs in abandoned buildings here, but only during the full moon. It's like a secret rave calendar that only locals seem to know.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows the difference between a burrito and a Mission-style burrito—it's all in the rice and the salsa, trust me.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a street performer on the N-Judah last week. He turned the whole car into an impromptu concert. Muni's chaos has a wild charm.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar has the same late-night vibe, but locals know the quiet gems are where you’ll find the best conversations and tapas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always struggle to navigate the layout of Gràcia's plazas, sticking to the main roads instead of wandering the vibrant alleys.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newcomers by their bewilderment at how many vegan cafes there are per square mile. It's like they're on a scavenger hunt.
Caught the 133 last night and the driver actually sang the route. Never seen that before. Kept us all entertained until Clapham!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid riding a bike in the middle of the street, dodging construction cones, while a dog chased after a pigeon. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see entire parks turning into open-air nightclubs, complete with DJ booths and makeshift bars. It's a weekend ritual.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their indignation over the bread selection at Tartine; tourists rave, but locals know the real gems are just a block away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver helping a lost tourist find their way to Dolores Park. Moments like these remind you that even in chaos, there's community.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think a siesta is just lying down for a nap. In Gràcia, it’s more about catching up with friends at the local bodega while the world slows down.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still look at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, squinting at each line with a puzzled frown.
